named James Buncker also came ashore in the New World. Having left his parents, the

zh.lifeSummary: 當James Bunker於大約1628年出生在英國戴文斯拉普頓時，他的父親James Bunker27歲，母親Elizabeth Bunker25歲。他於大約1662年與Sarah Nute在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1698年6月24日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾享年71歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Bunker于大约1628年出生在英国戴文斯拉普頓时，他的父亲James Bunker27岁，母亲Elizabeth Bunker25岁。他于大约1662年与Sarah Nute在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1698年6月24日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾享年71岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾。

nameMeanings.name: Bunker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname, of Norman origin, for a reliable or good-hearted person, from Old French bon ‘good’ + cuer ‘heart’ (from Latin cor).
History: Bunker Hill in Charlestown, MA, was named as land assigned in 1634 to George Bunker of Charlestown, who had emigrated from Odell in Bedfordshire, England.

variants.name: Banker
meanings: English: from Middle English banker, banc(o)ur, bankquer, bankert ‘covering of tapestry or other fabric for a bench, couch, or chair; ornamental hanging’, possibly a nickname or occupational name for someone who made or sold such things.
English: occupational name possibly from Middle English benker, seemingly ‘maker of benches’ but recorded only in names.
English: perhaps a variant of   Bunker  .

variants.name: Bunkers
meanings: Possibly also an altered form of English   Bunker  .
In some cases possibly also Dutch: perhaps a variant of Benkers, itself a cognate of German Benkert . The surname Bunkers is very rare in the Netherlands.

variants.name: Boenker
meanings: occupational name for a carpenter, a rounded form of   Benker  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When James Bunker was born about 1628, in Slapton, Devon, England, his father, James Bunker, was 27 and his mother, Elizabeth Bunker, was 25. He married Sarah Nute about 1662, in Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 24 June 1698, in New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 71, and was buried in New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Bunker est né environ 1628, à Slapton, Devon, Angleterre, son père, James Bunker, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Bunker, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Nute environ 1662, à Dover, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 24 juin 1698, à New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Bunker um 1628 in Slapton, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater James Bunker 27 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Bunker 25. Er hat um 1662 in Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America Sarah Nute geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 24. Juni 1698 im Alter von 71 Jahren in New Hampshire, British Colonial America und wurde in New Hampshire, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Bunker è nato il circa 1628 nel Slapton, Devon, England, il suo padre, James Bunker, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Bunker, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Nute circa 1662, nel Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 24 giugno 1698, nel New Hampshire, British Colonial America, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: James Bunkerが約1628年にイギリスデボンスラプトンで生まれた時，父James Bunkerは27歳，母Elizabeth Bunkerは25歳であった。約1662年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアドバーでSarah Nuteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1698年6月24日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアで71歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Bunker(이)가 대략 1628년 영국 데폰 슬라프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Bunker(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Bunker(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1662년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 도버에서 Sarah Nute(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1698년 6월 24일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Bunker nasceu aproximadamente 1628, em Slapton, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, James Bunker, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Bunker, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Nute aproximadamente 1662, em Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de junho de 1698, em New Hampshire, British Colonial America, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Bunker родился около 1628 г. в местности слаптон, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени James Bunker было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Bunker, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Nute около 1662 г. в местности довир, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 24 июня 1698 г. в местности ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Bunker nació aproximadamente 1628, en Slapton, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, James Bunker, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Bunker, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sarah Nute aproximadamente 1662, en Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 24 de junio de 1698, en New Hampshire, British Colonial America, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en New Hampshire, British Colonial America.
